Florence Foster Jenkins (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $27,383,770Details
International Box Office $28,616,569
Worldwide Box Office $56,000,339
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,556,945
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $504,179
Total $2,061,124
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The true story of the legendary New York heiress and socialite who obsessively pursued her dream of becoming a great singer. The voice she heard in her head was beautiful, but to everyone else it was hilariously awful. Her "husband" and manager, St. Clair Bayfield, an aristocratic English actor, was determined to protect his beloved Florence from the truth. But when Florence decided to give a public concert at Carnegie Hall, St. Clair knew he faced his greatest challenge.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,601,313 (24.1% of total gross)
Legs:4.15 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:48.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$29,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:1,528 opening theaters/1,528 max. theaters, 5.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $37,862,418

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 12th, 2016 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: April 29th, 2016 (Wide) (Australia)
May 6th, 2016 (Wide) (New Zealand)
May 6th, 2016 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
July 8th, 2016 (Wide) (Argentina)
July 8th, 2016 (Wide) (Brazil)
Video Release: November 29th, 2016 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for brief suggestive material.
(Rating bulletin 2435 (Cert #50258), 7/27/2016)
Running Time: 110 minutes
Geographical setting: New York City
Plot point: Biography, Celebrity Worship, Infidelity, Opera, Relationships Gone Wrong, Singers
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1940s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Pathe, Paramount Pictures, BBC Films, Canal Plus, Cine Plus, Qwerty Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

The true story of the legendary New York heiress and socialite who obsessively pursued her dream of becoming a great singer. The voice she heard in her head was beautiful, but to everyone else it was hilariously awful. Her "husband" and manager, St. Clair Bayfield, an aristocratic English actor, was determined to protect his beloved Florence from the truth. But when Florence decided to give a public concert at Carnegie Hall, St. Clair knew he faced his greatest challenge.
